metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. The only pain reference in the historical record mentions 'manual AP workload prevents analysis' but carries no cost, cycle time, error rate, headcount impact, or ROI figure. No Avoma discovery calls occurred to validate or quantify pain. Without metrics, Zycus cannot build a CFO-ready business justification or a defensible value case for a $100k P2P and supplier portal investment. The deal has spent 75 days in Qualified without this foundation, which is a structural gap blocking progression to any commercial stage. The immediate move is to secure a live discovery session with the relevant stakeholder the finance or procurement lead who owns the P2P pain, and extract quantified impact: AP invoice volume, cycle time, error/exception rate, FTE workload, and the cost of the status quo.

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Gianluca Pansera (Analyst, Evaluator) is the only contact, and there is no evidence of advocacy, access to the economic buyer, or ability to drive the internal agenda. Pansera has not viewed the demo videos 60 days after delivery despite committing twice (30 May: 'will get back in the next week'; 29 Jun: still not viewed), signaling disengagement or low priority. A champion in a P2P deal typically introduces the rep to the CFO or VP Finance, shares internal evaluation timelines, and pushes back on competitors on Zycus's behalf. None of this is happening. Pansera may be a coach (willing to share information) but is not a champion (active internal advocate with power). The deal is at high risk without a champion. The move is to ask Pansera in a live call this week: 'Who internally is driving the P2P and supplier-portal evaluation, and can you introduce us to them?' If Pansera cannot or will not make that introduction, escalate to the deal owner's manager (Michael McCarthy) for an executive connect.

competition.narrative: Competitive landscape is completely unknown. Competitor(s) field is empty, and no Avoma calls, Next Steps, Tasks, or Salesforce free-text fields reference rival vendors. It is unknown whether Estremar is sole-sourcing Zycus, running a formal RFP with multiple P2P and supplier-portal vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, Basware), or replacing an incumbent (and if so, who and why). The How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, and Replacing_What__c fields return no data. Without competitive intelligence, Zycus cannot position differentiation (Merlin agentic AI as the wedge vs legacy workflow automation), cannot counter a rival's pricing or narrative, and cannot assess win probability. For a $100k P2P deal in mid-2026, AI and Merlin should be the differentiation anchor if competitors are in play, but this positioning cannot happen in a vacuum. The move is to ask Pansera directly in the re-engagement call: 'Are you evaluating other vendors alongside Zycus, and if so, who?' and 'What are you using today for P2P and supplier management?'

identify_pain.narrative: Operational pain is mentioned in the historical record ('manual AP workload prevents analysis'), but it is not attributed to a named speaker, not dated, not quantified, and not confirmed on a live call. This is a second-hand reference with no grounding. The pain owner is unknown: is it Pansera's AP team, the finance VP, or the procurement lead? The pain is qualitative only (manual workload, inability to analyze), with no cost, FTE load, error rate, or cycle-time metric. For a P2P and supplier-portal deal, typical pain includes: invoice processing backlog, 3-way match exceptions, supplier onboarding delays, PO-to-invoice cycle time, and lack of spend visibility. None of these are confirmed for Estremar. The move is to run a discovery call this week and extract: (1) who owns the pain, (2) the quantified operational and financial impact, and (3) what happens if this is not solved (the 'do-nothing' cost).
